buggy_pattern: return err (raw gRPC error without errgrpc.ToNative in streaming proxy)
fixed_pattern: return errgrpc.ToNative(err)
sibling_location: core/events/proxy/remote_events.go — parallel proxy component in same PR; both remote_events.go and streaming.go fixed together

buggy_pattern: return nil // error from injectConnectFilters silently discarded in finalizePublicListenerFromConfig
fixed_pattern: return err
sibling_location: agent/xds/listeners.go finalizePublicListenerFromConfig — injectConnectTLSForPublicListener call site also returned nil on error; both fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: return nil // error from injectConnectTLSForPublicListener silently discarded
fixed_pattern: return err
sibling_location: agent/xds/listeners.go — injectConnectFilters call site was the structural twin; both error-swallowing returns fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: defer conn.Exec(ctx, "rollback", ...) in reloadTablesDataInDB — ctx cancellation silently kills rollback
fixed_pattern: reloadInTransaction() helper uses context.WithoutCancel to issue rollback on non-cancelable context
sibling_location: go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/schema/db.go reloadViewsDataInDB — identical defer rollback with cancelable ctx; fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: defer conn.Exec(ctx, "rollback", ...) in reloadViewsDataInDB — same cancelable-ctx rollback bug
fixed_pattern: reloadInTransaction() with non-cancelable context wrapping
sibling_location: go/vt/vttablet/tabletserver/schema/db.go reloadTablesDataInDB — the structural twin; both fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: width_bucket(operand, b1, b2, count): no guard for b1 == b2 case (division by zero / undefined per SQL spec)
fixed_pattern: if b1 == b2 { return nil, pgerror.New(pgcode.InvalidParameterValue, "lower bound cannot equal upper bound") }
sibling_location: pkg/sql/sem/builtins/math_builtins.go — both float8 and numeric overloads of width_bucket missing b1==b2 guard; both fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: width_bucket(operand, b1, b2, count): no guard for count <= 0 (undefined behavior, matches PostgreSQL error requirement)
fixed_pattern: if count <= 0 { return nil, pgerror.New(pgcode.InvalidParameterValue, "count must be greater than zero") }
sibling_location: pkg/sql/sem/builtins/math_builtins.go — both float8 and numeric overloads lacked count<=0 check; both fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: changeUserPassword: splits stmt on ';' with strings.Split without containsMultilineStatement() pre-check — multi-statement blocks silently mangled
fixed_pattern: if containsMultilineStatement(stmt) { if err := db.ExecuteTxQueryDirect(ctx, tx, stmt); err != nil { ... }; continue }
sibling_location: plugins/database/postgresql/postgresql.go changeUserExpiration — same ';'-split path without multiline guard; NewUser and customDeleteUser already had the check, making changeUserPassword/changeUserExpiration the unfixed pair

buggy_pattern: changeUserExpiration: same strings.Split-on-semicolon without containsMultilineStatement() check
fixed_pattern: if containsMultilineStatement(stmt) { ExecuteTxQueryDirect bypass; continue }
sibling_location: plugins/database/postgresql/postgresql.go changeUserPassword — structural twin; NewUser (line ~402) and customDeleteUser (line ~458) were the already-correct reference implementations verified at base SHA 9b752258

buggy_pattern: bo.Backoff() in Alloc() — no ctx param, no cancellation check; keeps retrying after context is done
fixed_pattern: if err := bo.Backoff(ctx); err != nil { return err }; if ctx.Err() != nil { return ctx.Err() }
sibling_location: pkg/meta/autoid/autoid_service.go Rebase() at line ~327 — identical bo.Backoff() without ctx; GetClient() also lacked ctx propagation; all three fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: bo.Backoff() in Rebase() — same missing ctx propagation, no context cancellation on backoff
fixed_pattern: bo.Backoff(ctx) with ctx.Err() check before resetConn call
sibling_location: pkg/meta/autoid/autoid_service.go Alloc() — structural twin; GetClient() was a third sibling; Backoff() signature changed to accept variadic ctx context.Context

buggy_pattern: index := pickFirstSampleIndex(floats, histograms) in funcResets — single index for dual-type series mishandles mixed native histograms
fixed_pattern: floatIndex, histIndex := pickFirstSampleIndices(floats, histograms) // separate indices per type
sibling_location: promql/engine.go funcChanges() — identical pickFirstSampleIndex call pattern; both funcResets and funcChanges fixed in same PR to use the new plural form

buggy_pattern: index := pickFirstSampleIndex(floats, histograms) in funcChanges — same single-index bug as funcResets
fixed_pattern: floatIndex, histIndex := pickFirstSampleIndices(floats, histograms)
sibling_location: promql/engine.go funcResets() — the structural twin; both functions have identical shape and were fixed together in PR #18906

buggy_pattern: validateVaultIssuerConfig: Vault auth path fields (AppRole.Path, Kubernetes.Path, AWS.MountPath, etc.) accepted without checking for '..' path-traversal segments
fixed_pattern: if containsDotDotSegment(path) { allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(fldPath, path, "must not contain '..' path segment")) }
sibling_location: internal/apis/certmanager/validation/issuer.go — 5 auth path fields (iss.Path, auth.AppRole.Path, auth.ClientCertificate.Path, auth.Kubernetes.Path, auth.AWS.MountPath) all added the same containsDotDotSegment check in same PR

buggy_pattern: /* Don't decref ap_t, ap_wrk, ap_iwrk - they are modified in-place */  // wrong: ap_wrk and ap_iwrk are temporary buffers not returned, must be decref'd
fixed_pattern: Py_DECREF(ap_wrk); Py_DECREF(ap_iwrk); /* Don't decref ap_t - it will be returned */
sibling_location: fitpack_percur() in same file: identical wrong comment and identical missing Py_DECREF(ap_wrk)/Py_DECREF(ap_iwrk) — copy-paste propagation; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: p.attn_bias_ptr + query_start * p.bias_strideM + iter_key_start  // query_start is uint32; product overflows for sequences > 2^32 / bias_stride
fixed_pattern: p.attn_bias_ptr + int64_t(query_start) * p.bias_strideM + iter_key_start
sibling_location: dropout skipahead index in same kernel function: (query_start + thread_i) * p.num_keys_absolute + iter_key_start — same u32 multiplication overflow; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: estimator.predict_proba(X[:, features], **(predict_params or {}))  // predict_proba received predict_params instead of predict_proba_params
fixed_pattern: estimator.predict_proba(X[:, features], **(predict_proba_params or {}))
sibling_location: estimator.predict() call in _parallel_predict_proba() same function: received predict_proba_params instead of predict_params — both dict names swapped; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: GGML_ASSERT(num_blocks < UINT_MAX);  // off-by-one: UINT_MAX is a valid uint, INT_MAX is the true ceiling before signed-cast overflow
fixed_pattern: GGML_ASSERT(num_blocks <= INT_MAX);
sibling_location: cpy_f32_q4_1, cpy_f32_q5_0, cpy_f32_q8_0, cpy_q8_0_f32 and other quantization copy functions in same file: same wrong < UINT_MAX assertion; all co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: ctx_dft.reset(llama_init_from_model(model_dft.get(), cparams));  // no null check; silently continues if model init failed
fixed_pattern: ctx_dft.reset(...); if (ctx_dft == nullptr) { SRV_ERR("%s", "failed to create draft context\n"); return false; }
sibling_location: server_batch::~server_batch() in same file: llama_batch_free(batch) called unconditionally without checking batch.token != nullptr first; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: sycl::native::exp(vals[col] - max_val)  // no lower bound clamp; native::exp on very negative values yields -inf/nan
fixed_pattern: sycl::native::exp(sycl::max(vals[col] - max_val, -80.0f))
sibling_location: sinks branch in same soft_max_f32() function: sycl::native::exp(sinks[i02] - max_val) had identical missing clamp; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: torch.is_autocast_enabled()  // missing device_type; torch.get_autocast_gpu_dtype() hardcoded to GPU path
fixed_pattern: torch.is_autocast_enabled(device_type); torch.get_autocast_dtype(device_type)
sibling_location: torch.amp.autocast(...) call in NemotronLayerNorm1P.forward() same file: also used hardcoded cuda device type, co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: hidden_states, _ = moe_kernel_quantize_input(A=hidden_states, ...); super().apply(...)  // super().apply() internally calls moe_kernel_quantize_input again → double quantization
fixed_pattern: // removed redundant moe_kernel_quantize_input() call; super().apply() handles quantization internally
sibling_location: vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/experts/ocp_mx_emulation_moe.py: identical extra moe_kernel_quantize_input() call before super().apply(); co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: // any/all reduction fell through to float dtype branch in _reduce(); result dtype incorrect for nullable boolean arrays with skipna=False
fixed_pattern: if name in ("any", "all"): np_dtype = "bool"  // explicit dtype selection before float branch
sibling_location: pandas/core/frame.py: if name == "all": skipna = True  // unconditionally overrode caller's skipna=False argument for groupby 'all'; co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: // Input(1, "frame_length") / Input(3, "frame_step") — comments and reads were reversed; signal_data + batch_idx * signal_size * signal_components  // multiplied by components count twice for complex input
fixed_pattern: // Input(1, "frame_step") / Input(3, "frame_length"); signal_data + batch_idx * signal_size + frame_start  // stride is already per-sample including both complex lanes
sibling_location: STFT::Compute() at line ~623 in same file: identical swapped Input(1)/Input(3) comments (frame_length vs frame_step); co-fixed in same PR

buggy_pattern: map: Option<[usize; 127]>  // i8 type IDs span 0..=127 which is 128 distinct values; array too short by one
fixed_pattern: map: Option<[usize; 128]>
sibling_location: let mut hash = [0; 127] in same function: identical off-by-one for the hash table indexing the same i8 domain; co-fixed in same PR
